article_meta.title: Supreme Court ruling against Trump’s tariffs is unlikely to mean an end to trade policy chaos 1 of 3 | Containers are st

reasoning: The model identifies several genuine bias instances in author prose but commits one attribution rule violation (the last item is a direct quote from Majerus) and uses non-standard bias type labels ('Loaded language / Subjective characterization' instead of the defined types like 'Word Choice', 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives', 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact'). Most flagged items are defensible instances of author-level bias, but there are some issues.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The last item ('The whole thing's going to be a mess') is a direct quote from Majerus - this is an attribution rule violation. The model even acknowledges 'without clear attribution to a source' but it IS attributed to Majerus in the article ('Majerus said'). The other 6 items are from author prose.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most items are defensible. 'stunning rebuke', 'conjure up on a whim', 'chaotic', 'strong-armed', 'lopsided', 'paralyzed' are all genuine loaded language in author prose. 'A messy refund process' as a subheading is borderline but defensible. The Majerus quote is a false positive due to attribution violation.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model caught many key instances. It could have also flagged 'sharp-elbowed tactics' and 'handy cudgel with which to smack countries' as loaded author language, and 'stunning rebuke' specifically (though it was partially captured in the larger phrase). Some bias by framing/slant in the overall negative tone could also be noted. Minor misses.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The model uses 'Loaded language / Subjective characterization' consistently, which is not one of the defined bias types. The correct labels would be 'Word Choice', 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives', or 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact' depending on the instance. This is a systematic labeling error - the model invented its own category instead of using the provided taxonomy.
